title: cyperus cunninghamii
intro: Cyperus cunninghamii, also known as the Kākābeak or Pōhutukawa sedge, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Māori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's roots and rhizomes are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, Cyperus cunninghamii is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and ointments for topical application.
